questions.question: A spaceship travels $240 \text{ km}$ in $4 \text{ hours}$. What is its unit rate?
A scientist mixes $2 \text{ liters}$ of a chemical with $5 \text{ liters}$ of water. What is the ratio of chemical to total mixture?
The cost of launching a satellite is directly proportional to its mass. If a $500 \text{ kg}$ satellite costs $$120,000$ to launch, how much will it cost to launch a $750 \text{ kg}$ satellite?
A rocket travels at a rate of $200 \text{ m/s}$. How far will it travel in $10 \text{ seconds}$?

The time it takes for a spacecraft to orbit the Earth is inversely proportional to its velocity. If a spacecraft orbits the Earth in $90 \text{ minutes}$ at a velocity of $7.8 \text{ km/s}$, how long will it take to orbit the Earth at a velocity of $10.2 \text{ km/s}$?
A lab experiment requires a ratio of $3:5$ of two chemicals. If $21 \text{ liters}$ of the first chemical are used, how many liters of the second chemical are needed?
A telescope can view a planet at a distance of $50 \text{ million km}$ in $10 \text{ seconds}$. How far away can it view the planet in $5 \text{ seconds}$?
A spacecraft travels $300 \text{ km}$ in $5 \text{ hours}$. What is its average speed in $\text{ km/h}$?

A lab experiment requires a mixture of two chemicals in a ratio of $2:3$. If $16 \text{ liters}$ of the first chemical are used, how many liters of the second chemical are needed? Show your work and calculate the total volume of the mixture.

The distance a spacecraft travels is directly proportional to the square of its velocity. If a spacecraft travels $1500 \text{ km}$ at a velocity of $20 \text{ m/s}$, how far will it travel at a velocity of $30 \text{ m/s}$? Show your work and explain your reasoning.

teacher_rules: Emphasize the importance of units and conversions in rate problems
Encourage students to use visual models to represent direct and inverse variation
Remind students to check their work and calculate carefully
